title: What Explains the Growing Divide Between Oil and Gas (Podcast)
summary: Gas and oil are on a sharply divergent path. In BloombergNEF’s Economic Transition Scenario, oil demand peaks before the end of the decade, while gas demand continues to grow as expanding power systems seek reliable sources of generation. At the same time, constraints on everything from gas turbine supply chains to grid infrastructure are emerging as critical bottlenecks. So how does the global energy system evolve from here, and what do these competing pressures reveal about what lies ahead for

title: Stocks Fall as Broadcom’s Outlook Fails to Inspire: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in high-flying chipmakers dragged down stocks, with Broadcom Inc.’s underwhelming outlook testing the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the market from war-driven lows.

title: Whale Rock Talks Up Anthropic’s User Prospects Ahead of IPO
summary: Whale Rock Capital Management founder and chief executive officer Alex Sacerdote said he expects Anthropic PBC to be headed towards half a billion users.

title: Goldman Erects Lobby Rockets as Morgan Stanley IPO Rivalry Heats Up
summary: No sooner had Goldman Sachs Group Inc. locked in top billing for the SpaceX IPO than Morgan Stanley dashed off a note to clients that it was actually co-lead on the deal, noting that their listing is in alphabetical order.

title: Blackstone’s BCRED Caps Redemptions After Investors Seek 10%
summary: Blackstone Inc. limited redemptions from its flagship private credit fund for the first time, after investors sought to pull 10% of the shares, the latest such fund to cap withdrawals amid a continued investor exodus.

title: SpaceX IPO Sells Rocket Business Hype in 17-Minute Video Pitch
summary: SpaceX set out its IPO pitch to retail investors early on Thursday with a video in which Chief Financial Officer Bret Johnsen joins the dots between the company’s rocket, satellite and AI businesses.

title: EU Delays Trading-Book Rules to Blunt Wall Street Advantage
summary: The European Union’s executive arm has unveiled what it hopes will be its final workaround to shield its lenders from being temporarily hit by harsher trading-book rules than those faced by Wall Street rivals.

title: Deadly Screwworm Parasite Found in US Threatens Cattle Herd
summary: A deadly cattle parasite was confirmed in the US for the first time in nearly a decade, posing the latest threat to a US herd already at its lowest level in 75 years.

title: Abu Dhabi’s IRH Turns Down Zambian Copper Concentrate Waiver
summary: The Zambian unit of Abu Dhabi’s International Resources Holding doesn’t plan to export copper concentrate, after again being granted the largest quota in a government waiver to ship the semi-processed form of the metal.

title: Buyout and Debt Funds Struggle With $632 Billion Stockpile
summary: Private markets firms have been slow to spend hoards of capital raised earlier this decade, sparking tough talks with investors about whether they should get more time to find investments.

title: Foreign Investors Snap Up Turkish Stocks in Fast-Track Trades
summary: Foreign investors are piling into fast-tracked sales of shares in Turkish companies, seeing pockets of value in a market facing political turmoil at home and fallout from the Iran war.

title: Morgan Stanley Sees Zambia Lift Eurobond Price to Break Impasse
summary: Morgan Stanley expects Zambia to revise its bond buyback offer higher in a bid to break an impasse with a group of bondholders resisting an initial price tabled almost a week ago.

title: Apollo’s Zelter Sees High-Grade Debt Sales Topping US Treasuries
summary: Investment-grade debt sales will outpace net issuance of US Treasuries this year, Apollo Global Management Inc.’s President Jim Zelter said, as the Magnificent Seven tech companies and others seek massive amounts of funding to fuel their expansion.

title: Oil Falls After Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Even as Clashes Persist
summary: Oil fell as a conditional ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon offered to ease the way toward a US-Iran peace deal, even as the truce was marred by ongoing clashes.

title: Nasdaq 100 Futures Drop as Broadcom Forecast Spooks Traders
summary: A selloff in technology stocks pushed US stocks lower before the bell on Thursday, as results from Broadcom Inc. forced an unwind in the artificial-intelligence trade that had powered gains over the last several sessions.

title: Sugar Futures Climb as Brazilian Mills Seen Making More Ethanol
summary: Raw sugar futures in New York advanced for the first time three days on expectations that Brazilian mills may allocate more cane to ethanol production, reducing sugar output during the current crushing season.

title: Dwindling US Oil Inventories Are a Warning to Global Markets
summary: The supply buffer is becoming uncomfortably thin as summer driving season begins.

title: Schneider Electric Banks on AI Boom to Sell €800 Million Debt
summary: Schneider Electric SE is planning to sell €800 million ($930 million) of debt following a powerful rally on the back of its increasing incursion into the business of supporting data centers.

title: Rand Swings From Carry Loser to Winner as Bond Inflows Surge
summary: South Africa’s rand is back near the top of the carry-trade table after languishing close to the bottom in the weeks following the outbreak of the Iran war.

title: US Premarket Movers for June 4, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.5% at 7:47 a.m. in New York as AI-linked stocks falter after a disappointing outlook from chipmaker Broadcom.

title: Economist Shah Says Texas Jobs Data Show Risks, Promise
summary: Sonal Shah, an economist and former chief executive officer of the Texas Tribune, says job growth in the state is picking up in 2026 after a stagnant 2025. But temporary employment accounts for an outsize share of the gains, and the US immigration crackdown is crimping labor supply, she tells Bloomberg's Julie Fine. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK and France Finalize Postwar Hormuz Mine-Clearing Mission
summary: The UK and France have finalized plans to lead a multinational mine-clearing mission in the Strait of Hormuz within days of an agreement between the US and Iran to reopen the waterway, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Zimbabwe Says China’s Huayou Plans Lithium Carbonate Plant
summary: China’s Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt Ltd. plans to set up a lithium carbonate plant in Zimbabwe, according to the southern African country’s mines minister.

title: Higher Losses Are Coming to Credit, Pimco’s Ivascyn Warns
summary: The first sustained default cycle in credit in many years has already started and the market will see higher losses than it’s grown used to, Pacific Investment Management Co.’s chief investment officer warned.

title: Cancer Drugmaker Parabilis Seeks $475 Million in US IPO
summary: Parabilis Medicines, a clinical-stage cancer drug developer, is seeking to raise $475 million in an initial public offering.

title: US Stocks Drag as Broadcom's Outlook Fails to Impress | Bloomberg Brief 6/4/2026
summary: Nasdaq 100 futures lead US equities lower as Broadcom's sales growth forecast failed to meet investors' lofty expectations. Iran says there had been no recent progress in peace deal talks with the US, while fighting persists in Lebanon. Sharon Bell of Goldman Sachs discusses the difference in sector exposure in the US and European equity markets. Rhonda Vonshay Sharpe of the Women's Institute for Science, Equity and Race focuses on the inequality in the labor market. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Denmark to Study Whether Wegovy Can Get More People Working
summary: The new government in Novo Nordisk A/S’s home country of Denmark wants to test whether weight-loss drugs can help get more people into work, adding a new economic dimension to the debate over obesity treatments.

title: The Mortgage Hedging ‘Beast’ Is Returning to the Treasury Market
summary: At the height of the US bond-market selloff last month, Vishal Khanduja detected something unusual.

title: Cerebras CEO on Delivering AI Inference at Scale
summary: Andrew Feldman, Co-Founder & CEO at Cerebras Systems discusses AI infrastructure and the economics of inference with Bloomberg’s Tom Giles at Bloomberg Tech 2026 in San Francisco. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Salesforce Sponsor Spotlight
summary: Madhav Thattai, Executive Vice President & General Manager, Agentforce at Salesforce speaks with Sameer Hassan, Chief Technology & Digital Officer at Williams-Sonoma about the latest in AI at Bloomberg Tech 2026 in San Francisco. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copper Climbs as Declining Inventories Offset Worries Over Iran
summary: Copper edged higher after a rise in orders for metal in London Metal Exchange warehouses, bucking a broader downtrend on the exchange as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s fraught push to reach a peace deal with Iran.
